---
title: "BCB744 Biostatistics"
author: "A. J. Smit"
date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY/MM/DD"
---

# Welcome

The biostatistics block moves from statistical foundations to model-based inference for biological and ecological questions.

The section is organised as a progression:

1. statistical thinking and exploratory data analysis,
2. core hypothesis tests,
3. regression and model construction,
4. dependence and model extensions, and
5. reproducible workflow.

Use this page as the section landing page, and the chapter menu for full navigation.

## Important Links

- [BCB744 Module Overview](../BCB744_index.qmd)
- [Biostatistics Self-Assessment](../tasks/BCB744_Biostats_Self-Assessment.qmd)
- [Biostatistics Example 1](../assessments/BCB744_BioStats_Example_1.qmd)
- [Course Synthesis](../../cheatsheets/cheatsheet-course-synthesis.pdf)
- [Statistics Methods Cheatsheet](../../cheatsheets/cheatsheet-inferential-stats.pdf)

## Back Matter

- [Subject Index (A–Z)](27-subject-index.qmd)

## Relationship to Intro R

This section assumes practical R and Quarto skills from the first half of the module.

- [Go to BCB744 Introduction to R](../intro_r/index.qmd)
